canonical.title: Standout dining in and around Ghent — 2026

siblings.title: Michelin & Gault&Millau-rated tables
siblings.summary: What's starred, Bib'd or top-scored in Ghent right now: 4 Michelin stars in city limits, a 2-star benchmark 30km away, and where Gault&Millau places everything else.

siblings.title: Chef-driven & destination tables
siblings.summary: The chefs to book for in Ghent, the drive-out destinations within ~90 minutes, and the 2024–2026 openings and closures that just redrew the map.

siblings.title: Romantic & special-occasion settings
siblings.summary: Six Ghent rooms worth a big night out — from a 24-seat Michelin star in a 1671 building to candlelit canal-side warehouses.

siblings.title: Cocktail-led dining & bar-restaurants
siblings.summary: Five Ghent venues where the drinks programme is at least as serious as the food — speakeasy, hotel-bar, canal-side, rooftop, Indian sharing-plate.

siblings.title: Distinctive cuisines beyond Franco-Belgian
siblings.summary: Standout non-Franco-Belgian restaurants in Ghent: omakase, ramen, Vietnamese rolls, Thai street food, Korean bibimbap, Pakistani tandoori, Syrian-Lebanese mezze, Moroccan tagines, Spanish tapas and Turkish kebab.

siblings.title: Remarkable locations & rooms
siblings.summary: Five Ghent venues where the room is the reason: a 15th-century butchers' hall, a converted Franciscan church, a restored 1880s warehouse, a tiled former butcher shop, and the city's two real rooftops.

siblings.title: Review & rating sources to mine
siblings.summary: Which review platforms to scrape for a 'clear edge' Ghent restaurant guide, ranked by signal density.
